The test passes immediately because the current implementation returns `[]` for any input, which trivially satisfies this test. This is an expected and valid TDD scenario - the test does not drive new behavior yet because the current "obvious implementation" (returning `[]`) already satisfies it.

In strict TDD, this is acceptable - the test confirms current behavior is correct for this case. The next test (3 live neighbors → reproduction) will be the one that forces the implementation to change.
